title: Single Cell Spatial Proteomics LC-MS/MS Analysis of Mouse Brain Tissue
author: Marion Pang

steps.title: Peptides are separated on an Aurora...
steps.instruction: Peptides are separated on an Aurora Ultimate UHPLC Column (25cm by 75µm), with column temperature maintained at 50 °C .

steps.title: 5 µL of sample from each...
steps.instruction: 5 µL of sample from each well is loaded onto the column.

steps.title: The separation gradient is configured with...
steps.instruction: The separation gradient is configured with a flow rate of 0.22 µL/min and an analytical duration of 01:00:00 . Further information of gradient conditions can be found in the supplementary information (Table S1 and S2).

steps.title: The LC system (Vanquish Neo UHPLC,...
steps.instruction: The LC system (Vanquish Neo UHPLC, Thermo Scientific) was coupled to an Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) with a Nanospray Flex ion source (Thermo Scientific).

steps.title: The LC-MS was configured for two...
steps.instruction: The LC-MS was configured for two types of data acquisition methods, which are detailed below.

steps.title: Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) was carried out...
steps.instruction: Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) was carried out in positive ion mode using a positive ion voltage of 1600 V while maintaining the ion transfer tube at a temperature of 300 °C . The maximum injection time was set to auto, and the normalized AGC target was set to 300%. Precursor ions with charges ranging from +2 to +6 were selectively targeted for fragmentation using a minimum intensity threshold of 5e3. Dynamic exclusion was set to exclude after one acquisition, with a 45-second exclusion duration and 10 ppm mass tolerance. MS2 scans were acquired in the Orbitrap at 60000 resolutions with an isolation window of 1.6 m/z, HCD collision energy set at 28%, and an auto-adjusted maximum injection time. The normalized AGC target was set at 200%. Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used for method implementation and data acquisition.

steps.title: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used...
steps.instruction: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used for method implementation and data acquisition.

steps.title: Retrieve samples from the -80 °C...
steps.instruction: Retrieve samples from the -80 °C freezer and allow to thaw till Room temperature .

steps.title: For best recovery of samples, add...
steps.instruction: For best recovery of samples, add 5 µL of lysis buffer ( 0.2 Mass / % volume n-Dodecyl-beta-Maltoside Detergent Thermo Fisher Catalog #89902 in 50 millimolar (mM) Triethylammonium Bicarbonate Thermo Scientific Catalog #90114 ) to each tube, then votex in an inverted position for 30s.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute .

steps.title: Transfer samples into a LoBind 384...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples into a LoBind 384 well PCR plate and incubate at 0 rpm, 70 °C, 1 hour in a PCR machine, with the lid set to 85 °C . LoBind 384 well PCR plate (Plate, Eppendorf, 0030129547) We have found that incubation using a tight-press PCR machine with a heated lid can help to increase the efficiency of sample lysis and protein denaturation.

steps.title: Following lysis, centrifuge the plate 1000...
steps.instruction: Following lysis, centrifuge the plate 1000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute and allow the plate to cool to room tempature.

steps.title: Add 1 µL of protease digestion...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of protease digestion mix consisting of 4 ng Trypsin, Mass Spectrometry Grade Promega Catalog #V5280 and 4 ng Lys-C, Mass Spectrometry Grade Wako Chemicals Catalog #125-05061 to each well.

steps.title: Incubate the plate at 37 °C Overnight
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at 37 °C Overnight .

steps.title: Centrifuge the plate at 1000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the plate at 1000 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute and quench digestion with 0.5 µL aqueous buffer comprising of 2 % volume Acetonitrile, LC-MS grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #51101 and 4 % volume Formic acid, LC-MS grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #28905 .

steps.title: The peptides are now ready for...
steps.instruction: The peptides are now ready for LC-MS/MS analysis, and can be stored in -80 °C until further processing.

steps.title: Following tissue processing and staining processes...
steps.instruction: Following tissue processing and staining processes (optional), tissue sections are mounted on a PET membrane metal frame slide. PET membrane (with metal frame), RNAse free (Slide, Leica, #76463-322, Proteomics (HPLC, Mass Spec) - no softeners, little autofluorescence, https://us.vwr.com/store/product/31049722/null ) 00:01:00

steps.title: Regions of interest were excised from...
steps.instruction: Regions of interest were excised from mounted tissues using a gravity-driven collection system by a LeicaLMD7000 or a Leica LMD7 (Leica Laser Microdissection Software Version V8.4). The laser settings are defined as below. A B C 1 Magnification 20X 40X 2 Power 55 24 3 Aperture 3 1 4 Speed 3 12 5 Specimen balance 15 12 6 Line spacing for Draw + Scan 15 19 7 Head current 100% 100% 8 Pulse frequency 1510 1046 9 Offset 115 180 Leica LMD7000 Laser Settings for 20x and 40x magnification. A B 1 Magnification 40X 2 Power 45 3 Aperture 1 4 Speed 25 5 Specimen balance 15 6 Line spacing for Draw + Scan 19 7 Head current 100% 8 Pulse frequency 519 9 Offset 180 Leica LMD7 Laser Settings for 20x and 40x magnification. Settings were optimized on 30um fixed mouse brain sections. Parameters should be optimized based on sample type.

steps.title: Collect the dissected tissue in the cap of a 0
steps.instruction: Collect the dissected tissue in the cap of a 0.65 mL Low Binding Tube containing 10 µL of lysis buffer consisting of 50 millimolar (mM) Triethylammonium Bicarbonate Thermo Scientific Catalog #90114 and 0.2 Mass / % volume n-Dodecyl-beta-Maltoside Thermo Scientific Catalog #89903 . Sorenson SafeSeal Low Binding Tube (Tube, Sorenson, 11300, 0.65mL) It is important to ensure that the buffer covers as much surface area of the cap as possible, to maximize odds of sample recovery. Other protein low binding tubes may be used, but must first be verified to fit in the sample holder of the LMD7000. The use of an anti-static gun may be beneficial for higher efficiency drop rates.

steps.title: Visually verify that the dissected tissue...
steps.instruction: Visually verify that the dissected tissue has landed on the cap.

steps.title: Vortex the tube, keeping it held...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube, keeping it held upside down for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes at 13000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes at 13000 x g, 25 °C, 1 minute .

steps.title: Store the samples at -80 °C until further processing
steps.instruction: Store the samples at -80 °C until further processing.

steps.title: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) was carried out...
steps.instruction: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) was carried out in positive ion mode using a positive ion voltage of 1600 V while maintaining the ion transfer tube at a temperature of 300°C. DIA settings were modified from Matzinger et al.(22) . Briefly, MS1 full scans were acquired with a range of 375-1200 m/z and a resolution of 120000. The maximum injection time was set to auto, and the normalized AGC target set to 300%. MS2 scans were acquired at 60000 resolutions with a maximum injection time of 118 ms and AGC target of 75%.

steps.title: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used...
steps.instruction: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used for method implementation and data acquisition.

steps.title: The steps below detail LC-MS/MS acquisition...
steps.instruction: The steps below detail LC-MS/MS acquisition on two separate LC-MS systems.

steps.title: Peptides are separated on an Aurora...
steps.instruction: Peptides are separated on an Aurora Ultimate UHPLC Column (25cm by 75µm), with column temperature maintained at 55 °C .

steps.title: 12 µL of sample from each...
steps.instruction: 12 µL of sample from each well is loaded onto the column.

steps.title: The separation gradient is configured with...
steps.instruction: The separation gradient is configured with a flow rate of 0.22 µL/min and an analytical duration of 00:20:00 . Further information of gradient conditions can be found in the supplementary information (Table S1 and S2).

steps.title: The LC system (Vanquish Neo UHPLC,...
steps.instruction: The LC system (Vanquish Neo UHPLC, Thermo Scientific) was coupled to an Orbitrap Astral mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) with a EasySpray ion source (Thermo Scientific).

steps.title: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) was carried out...
steps.instruction: Data-independent acquisition (DIA) was carried out in positive ion mode using a positive ion voltage of 1800 V while maintaining the ion transfer tube at a temperature of 275°C. full scans were acquired with a range of 400-800 m/z and a resolution of 240000. The maximum injection time was set to 100ms, and the normalized AGC target set to 500%. MS2 scans were acquired using 20m/z isolation windows across a precursor mass range of 400-800 m/z in a 0.6s duty cycle, with normalized HCD collision energy 25%, maximum injection time of 80 ms and AGC target of 800%.

steps.title: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used...
steps.instruction: Xcalibur software (Thermo Scientific) was used for method implementation and data acquisition.
